MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-23-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, OCT-23-2023. HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. THE COURT CONFIRMS THAT IT INTENDS TO ADOPT ITS TENTATIVE RULING. NO OBJECTION TO THE PRECEDING IS PRESENTED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. ***SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING***JUDGE: JOSEPH QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Kurtiss Jacobs, Esq., Hunt & Henriques, (669) 294-2158, for Plaintiff.

No other appearances.

RE: Plaintiff's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The court confirms that it intends to adopt its tentative ruling. No objection to the preceding is presented.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's claim of exemption is granted in part. The sheriff shall release the $2,467.18 it is currently holding to the plaintiff creditor. Any other sums shall be released to the defendant debtor. The levying officer shall garnish $250 per pay period (every 2 weeks) to service the plaintiff creditor's debt.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
